articles.title: COLLABORATION
articles.summary: When a dozen stakeholders need to get together to approve a campaign, it's hard to get things off the ground - and even harder to deliver quality work.

articles.title: Get the guide [4] or try Loom for free.
US ADVERTISING SPILL CAN MAKE A MESS
articles.summary: US advertising spill now risks damaging Canadian brands rather than helping them. Decades ago, American ads that reached Canadian audiences offered free exposure and often aligned closely enough with local positioning to be beneficial. Today, US brands promoting “America First” messaging create tension when those same brands present themselves as “Proudly Canadian” north of the border. When corporate campaigns in the US focus on nationalism, the spillover can undermine local credibility and make Canadian consumers feel excluded.

articles.title: YOUTUBE SHORTS NOW GENERATES MORE REVENUE PER WATCH HOUR THAN TRADITIONAL VIDEOS
articles.summary: YouTube Shorts now earns more revenue per watch hour than traditional videos. Strong ad performance, teen engagement, and higher conversion rates than TikTok have made Shorts a key growth driver alongside live sports and subscriptions. AI tools are improving content creation and shoppable video features, while direct response and retail ads led to a 15% rise in ad revenue.

articles.title: AGENTIC MARKETING: INBOUND IS JUST THE BEGINNING
articles.summary: Agentic Marketing redefines the funnel as a connected system where marketing stays involved across the buyer journey. AI agents qualify and route leads across inbound and outbound channels based on behavior and context, from form fills and email clicks to webinar activity and dormant CRM records. The divide between marketing, SDRs, and sales fades as agents manage real-time interactions and optimize follow-ups. Adoption often starts with one touchpoint, like live chat or post-event follow-up, before scaling to full-funnel orchestration.

articles.title: A CASE STUDY IN REDESIGNING NAVIGATION
articles.summary: The redesigned navigation for SFG20 increased SaaS demo requests by 38%. Simplifying the menu from 12 to 8 items improved usability and readability. The new design also led to a 35% increase in menu link clicks and a 12% rise in visits to key pages, including an 18% increase to “What is SFG20” and 52% to “Facilities-iQ.” The update reduced bounce rates and helped users better understand SFG20's offerings through clearer organization and storytelling.

articles.title: REGEX IN GSC REVEALS CHATGPT SEARCHES
articles.summary: ChatGPT and its browser Atlas rely on Google searches to answer prompts, producing longer, more detailed queries that often focus on pricing, reviews, and product comparisons. These queries can be detected in GSC using regular expressions to filter by length, transactional or brand terms, informational prompts, or listicle mentions. GA4 can identify pages driving the most ChatGPT traffic, which can then be linked to long-tail queries. Extensions like GSC Helper and Better Regex simplify saving, filtering, and exporting regex patterns to analyze AI-driven search activity.

articles.title: GOOGLE LOCAL SERVICES ADS VS. SEARCH ADS: WHICH DRIVES BETTER LOCAL LEADS?
articles.summary: Local businesses can generate leads through Google Ads using LSAs or traditional Search campaigns. LSAs offer quick setup with minimal management, relying on verification, accurate listings, and strong reviews. Google handles keyword matching, so tracking is key to gauge lead quality. Search campaigns provide greater control through custom copy, manual keywords, negatives, and bid strategies. They enable testing and segmentation but require more time and expertise.

articles.title: DIARY OF A BRAND: MONCLER
articles.summary: Moncler evolved from a technical outerwear maker to a luxury brand by tying function to aspiration. CEO Remo Ruffini drove this through selective distribution and high-impact fashion events that placed Moncler among top houses. Its signature puffed design and the Genius platform, now a cross-industry creative hub, deepened cultural relevance. Moncler expanded categories and achieved top-tier margins by prioritizing brand perception over short-term gains.

articles.title: HOW LOOM GREW TO 25M USERS WITHOUT TRADITIONAL MARKETING: PRODUCT-LED GROWTH MARKETING
articles.summary: Loom grew to 25M users without traditional marketing by making its product inherently shareable and easy to use. Its freemium model, Chrome extension, and built-in branding turned every shared video into a growth loop that brought in new users organically. The company's success came from smart positioning as a tool that enhances existing workflows rather than replaces them, appealing to knowledge workers in remote and hybrid setups. Its timing before the remote work boom and focus on friendly, simple design helped it become a $975M acquisition by Atlassian in 2023.

articles.title: BLUESKY REACHES 40M USERS—SO SHOULD YOUR BRAND BE ON IT?
articles.summary: Brands should explore Bluesky if their audience aligns with its active niche communities, though it's not suited for broad reach or ads yet.

articles.title: IS THE HEAD OF CEO CONTENT ROLE REAL OR BS?
articles.summary: “Head of CEO Content” roles usually fail as defined, but can succeed when focused on storytelling and ‘domain knowledge' to showcase the CEO's voice.
